para.as_written: ܐܬܕܟܪ ܡܪܝܐ ܝܘܣܦ ܟܬ>ܘ<ܒܘܢܐ ܒܡܠܘܬܟ / ܪܝܫܐ ܕܐܪܟܢ ܒܟܬܒ̈ܝܟ. ܢܬܪܡܪܡ ܒܝܘܡ ܡܬܝܬܟ ܘܥܝ̈ܢܐ ܕܬܪ̈ܨܝ ܦܬܓܡܝܟ܆ ܢܬܦܫ̈ܛܢ ܠܘܬ ܫܘܒܩܢܐ܆ ܘܐܝܕ̈ܝܐ ܕܪܫ̈ܡܝ ܡܐܡܪܝܟ܆ ܢܚܙܝ̈ܢ ܫܘܒܚܐ ܕܡܠܟܘܬܟ / ܐܬܕܟܪ ܡܪܝܐ ܠܙܝܢܘܢ ܩܫܝܫܐ ܩܢܝܐ ܕܟܬܒܐ ܒܡܠܟܘ(ܬܟ)
translation: Remember, oh Lord, the poor scribe Joseph in your kingdom. Let the head that inclined over your Scriptures be raised on the day of your Coming; and the eyes that stared at your Words to be stretched to forgiveness; and the hands that wrote your Words to see the Glory of your Kingdom. Remember, oh Lord, the priest Zīnōn, the possessor of the book, in your Kingdom.

para.as_written: ܐܫܬܡܠܝ ܟܬܒܐ ܗܢܐ ܕܐܘܢܓܠܝܘܢ ܩܕ(ܝܫܐ) ܒܟ̄ܒ̄ ܡܢ ܬܫܪܝܢ ܒ̄ ܠܐܦܝ ܬܫܥ ܫܥܝ̈ܢ ܝܘܡ ܬܠܬܐ ܒܫܒܐ܀ ܫܢܬ ܐܠܦ ܘܐܪܒܥܡܐ ܠ̄ ܛ̄ ܡܢ ܫܢܝ̈ܐ ܕܐܠܟܣܢܕܪܘܣ ܡܩܕܘܢܝܐ ܟܬܒܗ ܕܝܢ ܡܚܝܠܐ ܘܚܛܝܐ ܘܒܨܝܪܐ ܕܟܠܗܘܢ ܒܢܝ̈ܢܫܐ ܝܘܣܦ ܚܛܝܐ ܡܢ ܩܪܝܬܐ ܢܛܪܬ ܒܐܠܗܐ ܩܐܪܐ ܐܡܝܢ ܟܠ ܕܩ̇ܪܐ ܢܨܠܐ ܥܠ ܚܛܝܐ ܕܣܪܛ
translation: This book of the Holy Evangelion was completed on the 22nd of Teshrin II, about the ninth hour on the third day of the week; [in] the year one thousand four hundred thirty-nine from the years of Alexander the Macedonian. The weak, sinful and the [most] unworthy of all the people, the sinner Joseph from the village Qara, protected by God, copied it. Amen. Let everybody who reads [this book] pray for the sinner who scribed.

assoc_date.as_written: ܟ̄ܒ̄ ܡܢ ܬܫܪܝܢ ܒ̄ ܠܐܦܝ ܬܫܥ ܫܥܝ̈ܢ ܝܘܡ ܬܠܬܐ ܒܫܒܐ܀ ܫܢܬ ܐܠܦ ܘܐܪܒܥܡܐ ܠ̄ ܛ̄ ܡܢ ܫܢܝ̈ܐ ܕܐܠܟܣܢܕܪܘܣ ܡܩܕܘܢܝܐ
note: 22nd of Teshrin II, about the ninth hour on the third day of the week; [in] the year one thousand four hundred thirty-nine from the years of Alexander the Macedonian (1439 AG = 1127 CE)

bib.citation: Kāmil, Murād. Catalogue of All Manuscripts in the Monastery of St. Catherine on Mount Sinai. Harrassowitz, 1970
Manuscripts in St. Catherine's Monastery, Mount Sinai. Library of Congress

program.label: NEH Syriac Parchment Descriptions Grant
program.description: Catalogued as part of UCLA Library's "Identification and Description of the Syriac and Arabic Manuscripts at St. Catherine’s Monastery of the Sinai, Phase 1: Syriac Parchment Manuscripts" project (2022-2025), funded by the National Endowment for the Humanities. Principle cataloguers were Grigory Kessel (Lead cataloguer), Natalia Smelova (Associate cataloguer), and Vevian Zaki (Cataloguer for Arabic content in Syriac manuscripts).
